system_meaning: ability to absorb disturbance without major function loss
ability to restore function after disruption
common_tradeoff: fast restoration may preserve fragile structures

system_meaning: ability to learn and adjust behavior rules or resources
ability to shift to a new viable configuration
backup capacity alternate routes and overlapping functions
common_tradeoff: redundancy can be expensive or underused

system_meaning: variation in components strategies and knowledge
ability to contain failure within parts of the system
common_tradeoff: too much modularity can reduce integration

system_meaning: ability to retain lessons and update behavior
common_tradeoff: past lessons can become rigid assumptions
